proof_limits: compose_building assembles caller / COO declared node and edge choices only

deferred_smith_review_queue: Composition decision: this Building ran plan -> review -> closure with no dedicated WORK/investigation node; the file:line investigation emerged across the review and closure reads rather than from one owned node. Confirm whether that is the intended shape for read-only proof Buildings.

The principle 'adapter = full-power connector; YAML/tool_policy/write_scope = control' is proven by code structure only; the live write-admissibility half remains a not_proven prediction pending a deliberately declared write-scope live run.
narrowly_proven: Principle (structure level): the adapter is a full-power connector and YAML/tool_policy/write_scope are the control. adapter:claude-local capability = {READ, WRITE, WEB} (adapter_constants.py:96-98) yet effective write requires the three-term intersection (agent_adapter.py:868-877); capability alone never opens write.
Seat (a) adapter capability — CONFIRMED NOT the cap: claude-local is write-capable and is in _OBSERVED_WRITE_ADAPTER_REFS (adapter_constants.py:75-98).

Seat (c) Agent YAML/tool_policy — CONFIRMED a control gate: this Building carries tool-policy:leader-coordination, not READ_WRITE_TOOL_POLICY_REF, so the second effective_write term is False (agent_adapter.py:875; adapter_constants.py:26).

Seat (d) Brick write_scope NEED absence — CONFIRMED a control gate: write_scope={} makes bool(request.write_scope) False (agent_adapter.py:874), so effective_write is False and read_tier opens (agent_adapter.py:880-911).

Prediction 'read-only Brick -> read-only projected tools' is proven by code structure: with no write_scope and a read-tier policy, effective_write=False and the read browse tier opens for claude-local.

The carried plan node was return-shape complete and boundary-clean, and the carried review reproduced the structural intersection independently — the parent objective's file:line evidence exists across the carried AgentFacts plus this closure.
next_target_candidates: brick:write-scope-live-run-proof — a declared write-scope Brick with tool-policy:read-write-scoped on adapter:claude-local to observe effective_write at runtime.
brick:agent-resources-yaml-body-coverage — read-only sweep of agent_resources.py write-tool projection and each tool_policy/object YAML body for lane-by-lane confirmation.
brick:provider-sandbox-read-denial-investigation — localize the plan-session read-content denial origin.
link/COO composition review — decide whether investigation Buildings of this kind should carry a dedicated WORK node.
not_proven: Seat (b) provider sandbox/env isolation as the read-only ORIGIN — NOT a code seat and not reproduced: the plan session's Read/Grep content denial was a per-session permission state; the review and this closure session had read access. Its origin (env isolation vs session permission grant) is a runtime/environment constraint, not adapter design.

Prediction 'write-scope Brick + write-capable lane (tool-policy:read-write-scoped) + aligned observed-write adapter -> admissible write' is structure-consistent (all three intersection terms can be satisfied) but NOT confirmed at runtime — no live Claude write run was performed (task-source excludes it).

Whether agent_resources.py tool projection and each individual tool_policy YAML body fully match this intersection rule — the rule was confirmed in agent_adapter.py / adapter_constants.py; agent_resources.py write-tool projection and per-file YAML bodies were not exhaustively opened here.
Checker/--all status — not run (task-source excludes); no claim.
Whether the absence of a dedicated WORK/investigation node in the plan->review->closure chain is intended — a Link/COO composition decision, not observed truth.
brain surface behavior, credential validity, tool or hook execution, runtime/scheduler behavior, quality of returned work.
Source truth, success, quality, approval, and Movement — outside this closure's authority.
observed_evidence: adapter_constants.py:90-98 — _ADAPTER_CAPABILITIES[ADAPTER_CLAUDE_LOCAL] = {READ, WRITE, WEB}; the claude-local connector technically carries WRITE, so the adapter does NOT cap Claude to read-only.
adapter_constants.py:75-77 — _OBSERVED_WRITE_ADAPTER_REFS = {codex-local, codex-fugu-local, claude-local}; claude-local IS an observed-write adapter.
agent_adapter.py:1131-1132 — _adapter_ref_supports_observed_write(adapter_ref) returns adapter_ref in _OBSERVED_WRITE_ADAPTER_REFS, so it is True for adapter:claude-local.

agent_adapter.py:868-877 — agent_request_effective_write() = bool(request.write_scope) AND READ_WRITE_TOOL_POLICY_REF in request.tool_policy_refs AND _adapter_ref_supports_observed_write(adapter_ref): the exact 'write_scope NEED AND read-write tool-policy AND observed-write adapter' intersection the task asked to locate.

agent_adapter.py:880-911 — agent_request_read_tier(): a non-effective-write request carrying a known tool-bearing policy opens the read-only browse tier for codex/codex-fugu/claude/gemini local; effective-write requests early-return False (write path).

agent_adapter.py:297-329 — adapter_capabilities()/adapter_has_capability()/adapter_is_write_capable() read technical capability from _ADAPTER_CAPABILITIES; capability is a connector property separate from the effective-write gate.

adapter_constants.py:26 — READ_WRITE_TOOL_POLICY_REF = 'tool-policy:read-write-scoped'; this Building's packet carries tool_policy_refs=[tool-policy:leader-coordination] (read-tier), NOT read-write-scoped.

This closure packet: write_scope={} (empty) and native_grant.write_effective=false — both effective_write terms (non-empty write_scope, read-write tool-policy) fail, so the request resolves to the read tier.

The seat of read-only behavior for this Building is the **control layer (empty Brick write_scope + non-read-write tool-policy)**, NOT the adapter: `adapter:claude-local` is declared write-capable as a connector. If exhaustive structural coverage is wanted, read support/connection/agent_resources.py write-tool projection and each agent/tool_policies/*.yaml + agent/objects/*.yaml body to confirm lane-by-lane which carry read-write-scoped vs reviewer-readonly.

Investigate the plan-session read-content denial separately (provider sandbox/session-permission), since it was non-reproducible and is the only unresolved 'read-only' phenomenon.
